subject: Human Resources
title: Ergonomics and Repetitive Strain
description: A fast breakdown of Ergonomics and Repetitive Strain for builders.

hook.text: Musculoskeletal disorders account for nearly 30% of all workers' compensation claims. Sitting wrong costs billions.

buildup.text: Ergonomics designs workspaces to fit human bodies—preventing injuries from repetitive motions, poor posture, and strain.

discovery.text: Common issues: carpal tunnel from typing, back pain from sitting, and shoulder injuries from repetitive overhead work.

twist.text: OSHA has no specific ergonomics standard—it was withdrawn in 2001. Employers use the General Duty Clause instead.

climax.text: Proactive ergonomic assessments—adjusting desks, chairs, and workflows—prevent injuries far cheaper than treating them.

punchline.text: A $500 chair saves a $50,000 injury claim. Prevention is the best investment.

quiz.question: Why is ergonomics important in the workplace?
options: Musculoskeletal disorders account for nearly 30% of workers' comp claims
It's legally required by a specific OSHA standard
